body:
## Why this matters

#52 explicitly calls for storage observability sourced from `projectbluefin/bluespeed#7`, but the current queue only defines the role matrix (#57) and the shared-storage blocker (#62). Without a dedicated observability issue, storage validation will prove mounts and persistence loosely while missing the read-only signals that show whether those role assumptions are actually healthy.

## Scope

Define the storage observability follow-up under #52:

- identify the minimum read-only storage signals the repo must surface for role validation: mounted filesystems, capacity/headroom, pool or device health, and error conditions that materially affect persistence claims
- state where ZFS-specific signals belong versus generic filesystem and mount evidence so the lane does not assume every role is ZFS-backed
- define how observability evidence should support reboot/update/persistence validation instead of becoming a separate monitoring project
- link any shared-storage or restore dependencies that cannot be validated until the observability surface exists

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The minimum storage observability surface is defined for NAS, media-server, and general-purpose-server validation
- [ ] ZFS-specific versus generic storage signals are explicit
- [ ] The issue states how observability evidence supports reboot/update persistence claims
- [ ] Dependencies on #57, #62, and downstream restore/service work are linked directly

## Links

- Child of #52
- Depends on #57 and #54
- Informs #55 and #51
- Source ideas: `projectbluefin/bluespeed#7`
title: feat: define ZFS/storage observability lane for homelab role validation
